title: The Timetables of History: A Horizontal Linkage of People and Events
excerpt: A comprehensive chronological reference linking world events across categories — history, literature, science, art, and daily life — from 5000 BCE to the present in parallel timelines.
keyIdeas: History is best understood by seeing simultaneous events across different domains
Parallel timelines reveal connections between political, cultural, scientific, and everyday developments
Chronological reference enables readers to understand what was happening in context across the world
keyTakeaways: The book covers 7,000 years of history from 5000 BCE to 1990 CE
Events are organized into seven categories displayed in parallel columns
The layout allows readers to see what was happening simultaneously in different fields
Categories include politics, literature, religion, science, art, music, and daily life
An index of names and subjects makes it easy to find specific events
whoShouldRead: History enthusiasts wanting to understand chronological relationships
Students preparing for exams requiring chronological knowledge
Writers and researchers needing to contextualize historical events
Anyone who enjoys trivia and understanding how events relate in time
whoShouldSkip: Readers seeking narrative history rather than reference format
Those who prefer deep analysis of specific periods
People who find tabular reference works visually unappealing
